narrative:
Trea Turner and Christian Yelich anchor today’s offensive profile, boasting the highest projected outputs at 84.41 and 83.18, respectively. Turner’s lead-off efficiency against Foster Griffin is bolstered by his speed advantage, while Yelich leverages a plus-matchup against Nick Martinez and a strong historical BvP edge. Junior Caminero and Kazuma Okamoto remain essential cornerstones, with both projected to hit in the cleanup spot and capitalize on favorable SP skill and order-slot leverage to maximize our run production and RBI potential.

Despite the strength at the top of the order, Yordan Alvarez and Kyle Schwarber face stiff L/L platoon penalties against Suarez and Griffin, capping their upside despite their high-end xwOBA profiles. We are keeping a close eye on the Logan O'Hoppe and Dillon Dingler platoon situations; both catchers are battling low-order lineup projections that dampen their base scores. With Caleb Durbin benefiting from a significant skill-based advantage against McCullers Jr., we are prioritizing these situational matchups to maintain 5x5 Roto efficiency in a day defined by high volatility at the bottom of the batting order.
